title: cydonia vulgaris silvestris
intro: Cydonia vulgaris silvestris, also known as the wild medlar, is a species of deciduous shrub or small tree in the family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cydonia vulgaris silvestris include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
